notes: Spec asserts the session stays solidly in working across opencode's incremental assistant output and does NOT flicker to ready before the terminal turn-end signal. For opencode the streamed unit is the assistant text part: parseTextPart maps every assistant text part to assistant_message (parser.go:178-197, no turn boundary, excluded from IsAgentDone), and step-finish reason=tool-calls also maps to assistant_message (parser.go:155-157) — only step-finish reason=stop emits turn_done (parser.go:152-154) and settles working→ready. The daemon observes the SQLite part rows via the WAL-fsnotify-driven ProcessOwnedStore watcher (scanParts, time_updated cursor) as a UUID-keyed ses_… session. The streaming_window phase pins the no-flicker invariant: no premature →ready and no →waiting (tool_calls_max:0) across the streamed flushes. session_birth is anchored to recording start and left UNPINNED so a transient proc-<PID> presession cannot steal identity and cascade same_session_as failures; turn1_start (matching working) establishes the session identity the later phases chain off. OBSERVABILITY CAVEAT: a no-tool prose turn driven headlessly via `opencode run` can flush all of its assistant text + the terminal step-finish to the WAL in one scan window at turn completion (basic-turn collapsed working→ready in ~0.3ms for this reason); the multi-line prompt aims to spread the text parts across multiple WAL flushes so the working dwell is genuinely observable, but if opencode writes the whole reply atomically the streaming_window has nothing intermediate to assert over — flag as observability_correction rather than rebasing the spec.

text: Session appears in ready within 2s of agent launch; never enters waiting (no tool use)
User prompt for a multi-line response drives the ses_… session ready → working

During streaming the session stays solidly in working. opencode's incremental assistant text parts land as assistant_message (non-terminal); none must promote to ready and no waiting cue fires before the terminal step-finish reason=stop.
Terminal step-finish reason=stop lands; the turn completes back to ready on the same ses_… session
